title: American Buttermilk-Style Fried Chicken (Kadai Method)

ingredients.name: chicken pieces (drumsticks and thighs with bone)

subs.name: cauliflower florets for a vegetarian version; reduce frying time to 6–8 minutes

ingredients.name: buttermilk (chaas / thick mor)
visual_checks: marinate 1–2 hours or overnight for best results; even 15 min works
ingredients.notes: marinate 1–2 hours or overnight for best results; even 15 min works

subs.name: 1 cup plain curd (dahi) thinned with 2 tbsp water — works identically as a tenderising marinade
50:50 maida and cornflour for an even crispier crust; cornflour reduces gluten for a more shattering texture

steps.text: Score the chicken pieces two or three times down to the bone so the marinade penetrates. Add the garlic, half the chilli powder, half the pepper, salt and buttermilk. Mix well to coat every surface. Marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to overnight in the refrigerator.
Combine the maida with the remaining chilli powder, pepper and a generous pinch of salt in a wide flat dish. Mix thoroughly.

Lift each chicken piece from the buttermilk marinade, letting the excess drip off but keeping the coating wet. Dredge it firmly in the seasoned maida, pressing the flour into every crevice. Set on a plate and rest 5 minutes so the coating adheres.
Heat oil in a kadai to 170°C — a pinch of flour should sizzle steadily without burning immediately. Slide in 2–3 pieces at a time, keeping the oil temperature steady by not crowding the pan.

Fry bone-in thighs and drumsticks for 12–14 minutes, turning once halfway, until the crust is deep golden and the juices run clear when a skewer is pushed to the bone. The internal temperature should reach 75°C.
Drain on a wire rack or paper-lined plate — do not cover or the crust will steam and soften. Rest for 5 minutes before serving. Serve hot with coleslaw and ketchup.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: buttermilk → 1 cup plain curd (dahi) thinned with 2 tbsp water — works identically as a tenderising marinade; maida → 50:50 maida and cornflour for an even crispier crust; cornflour reduces gluten for a more shattering texture; chicken → cauliflower florets for a vegetarian version; reduce frying time to 6–8 minutes
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Chaas or plain thin mor (buttermilk) is the perfect substitute for American buttermilk — same acidity, same tenderising effect. Use a deep kadai with enough oil to submerge the pieces by at least half. Maintain medium-high heat throughout. Garam masala (half a tsp) can be added to the flour coating for a desi twist.
